reasoning: Peers are Denzi dungeon tiles — cool stone grays, teal/cyan accents, dark reds, gold, bone white, with gritty high-detail pixel texture. (2) Outline color: brown frame tones partially align, but the green teal corner studs read more decorative than the peers' structural teal. (3) Shadow direction: dungeon tiles show ambient/no directional shadow; candidate card has explicit drop-shadow border implying top-left light source — inconsistent treatment. (4) Shadow count: peers 0-1 subtle shadows; candidate has a multi-layer shadow/border effect. (7) Detail density: peers pack cracks, vines, stains, cross-hatching at high density; candidate is nearly flat and sparse by comparison. (8) Proportion: rectangular card vs square tiles — different item type, not penalized per rules. (9) Rendering style: both pixel art, but peers are gritty/environmental; candidate is clean/UI-friendly — aesthetic register does not match. (10) Palette overlap: teal corner pieces connect, but the dominant beige/cream and soft pink/flesh tones have no anchor in the peer set. (11) Charm level: peers are neutral-grim dungeon assets; candidate reads softer and friendlier, misaligning affective register. Multiple hard failures in temperature, palette, and density place this below the marginal band.
